content.problemStatement:
You are given a grid with NNN rows and MMM columns; each cell of this grid is either empty or contains an obstacle. Initially, all cells are colorless.

You may perform the following operation an arbitrary number of times (including zero): choose a 2×22\times 22×2 square in the grid and color all four of its cells. Each cell may be colored any number of times.

You want all empty cells to be colored and all cells containing obstacles to be colorless. Find out if this state can be achieved.
constraints: the sum of N⋅MN\cdot MN⋅M over all test cases does not exceed 5,000,0005,000,0005,000,000
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (30 points):** only the first row of the grid may contain obstacles
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
The following NNN lines describe the grid. Each of these lines contains a string with length MMM containing only characters '.' (denoting an empty cell) and '#' (denoting a cell with an obstacle).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the string `"YES"` if it possible to color the grid in the required way or `"NO"` if it is impossible (without quotes).
